quotes.text: Ann bought me a waffle iron for my birthday, which was thrilling until I realized you actually have to make the batter. I thought it was like a toaster.
quotes.speaker: Mitt Romney
quotes.context: Late Night with Seth Meyers interview, 2015
quotes.fake_note: Matches his famously mild, slightly out-of-touch dad persona perfectly. It sounds entirely plausible, but it is just a bit too perfectly sitcom-esque for him to have actually admitted on late-night TV.

quotes.text: The trick to painting a dog's nose is you can't just use black. You gotta mix in a little purple. It sounds crazy, but otherwise they look like they're wearing a fake mustache.
quotes.speaker: George W. Bush
quotes.fake_note: Fits perfectly with his post-presidency painting hobby and his folksy, self-deprecating delivery. It sounds like genuine art advice, but is entirely fabricated.

quotes.text: I will do it myself where does he live?

quotes.text: Look, let me be clear. I’m not saying I invented the concept of mixing popcorn and Milk Duds at the movies, but I definitely popularized it in the greater Chicago area.
quotes.speaker: Barack Obama
quotes.fake_note: Nails his classic 'let me be clear' rhetorical framing applied to a highly trivial, relatable topic. The tell is the hyper-specific geographic boast about a common movie theater habit.

quotes.text: I have said it before and I will say it again: there is absolutely no reason a grocery store needs fourteen different types of vanilla extract. Pure, imitation, Mexican, Madagascar—just pick one and let us go home.
quotes.speaker: Bernie Sanders
quotes.fake_note: Captures his signature repetitive cadence and frustration with consumer excess. The tell is that he famously focuses on the abundance of deodorant choices or sneakers, not baking supplies.

quotes.text: The Internet is not something that you just dump something on. It's not a big truck. It's a series of tubes.
quotes.speaker: Ted Stevens
